approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: The module supports ECDSA P-384 and P-521 digital certificate authentication of Peers for

The module supports the use of a pre-Shared key for Datapath Encryption peer authentication for Users; The Pre-Shared key is 32 bytes in length. Using conservative estimates, the probability for a random attempt to succeed is: 1:2^256 or 1: 1.579 × 10^77 which is less than 1:1,000,000. The fastest network connection supported by the modules over the management interface is 10 MBits/s; Hence, at most 10 ×10^6 × 60 = 6 × 10^8 = 600,000,000 bits of data can be transmitted in one minute;

The module supports ECDSA digital certificate authentication of COs over the TLS 1.; Using conservative estimates and equating the use of ECDSA with P-521 elliptic curve to a 256- bit strength, the probability for a random attempt to succeed is: 1:2^256 or 1: 1.579 × 10^77 which is less than 1:1,000,000 The fastest network connection supported by the modules over Management interfaces is 2.5 GB/s; Hence, at most 2.5 ×10^9 × 60 = 15 × 10^10 = 150,000,000,000 bits of data can be transmitted in one minute Therefore, the probability that a random attempt will succeed, or a false acceptance will occur in one minute is: 1: (2^256 possible keys / ((15 × 10^10 bits per minute) / 256 bits per key)) 1: (2^256 possible keys / 585,937,500 keys per minute) 1: 1.97 × 10^68 which is less than 1:100,000 within one minute

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.keySize: 0.5/bit

Datapath Encryption/ Decryption Service (Perform approved security functions)
Report the firmware version, alarms, Status and Statistics (Show status, Show module’s versioning information)
The module supports ECDSA P-384 and P-521 digital certificate authentication of Peers for

Datapath Encryption. Using conservative estimates and equating the use of ECDSA P-384- bit certificate to 128 bits of security, the probability for a random attempt to succeed is: 1:2^192 or 1: 6.277 x 10^57 which is less than 1:1,000,000. The fastest network connection supported by the modules over Management interfaces is 10MBits/s; Hence, at most 10 ×10^6 × 60 = 6 × 10^8 = 600,000,000 bits of data can be transmitted in one minute. Therefore, the probability that a random attempt will succeed, or a false acceptance will occur in one minute is: 1: (2^192 possible keys) / ((6 × 10^8 bits per minute) / 192 bits per key)) 1: (2^192 possible keys / 3125000 keys per minute) 1: (2 × 10^51) which is less than 1:100,000 within one minute

The module supports the use of a pre-Shared key for Datapath Encryption peer authentication for Users; The Pre-Shared key is 32 bytes in length. Using conservative estimates, the probability for a random attempt to succeed is: 1:2^256 or 1: 1.579 × 10^77 which is less than 1:1,000,000. The fastest network connection supported by the modules over the management interface is 10 MBits/s; Hence, at most 10 ×10^6 × 60 = 6 × 10^8 = 600,000,000 bits of data can be transmitted in one minute;

Therefore, the probability that a random attempt will succeed, or a false acceptance will occur in one minute is: 1: (2^256 possible keys / ((6 × 10^8 bits per minute) / 256 bits per key)) 1: (2^256 possible keys / 2,343,750 keys per minute) 1: 4.94 × 10^70 which is less than 1:100,000 within one minute
Initial Device ID (iDevID) and Local Device ID (LDevID) Public Key

The module supports ECDSA digital certificate authentication of COs over the TLS 1.; Using conservative estimates and equating the use of ECDSA with P-521 elliptic curve to a 256- bit strength, the probability for a random attempt to succeed is: 1:2^256 or 1: 1.579 × 10^77 which is less than 1:1,000,000 The fastest network connection supported by the modules over Management interfaces is 2.5 GB/s; Hence, at most 2.5 ×10^9 × 60 = 15 × 10^10 = 150,000,000,000 bits of data can be transmitted in one minute Therefore, the probability that a random attempt will succeed, or a false acceptance will occur in one minute is: 1: (2^256 possible keys / ((15 × 10^10 bits per minute) / 256 bits per key)) 1: (2^256 possible keys / 585,937,500 keys per minute) 1: 1.97 × 10^68 which is less than 1:100,000 within one minute

Periodic inspection of tamper- evident seals when moving/replacing the module

Two tamper-evident seals are applied to the multi-chip embedded cryptographic module during manufacturing; The physical security of the module is intact if there is no evidence of tampering with the tamper-evident seal(s); If evidence of tamper is found, the Cryptographic Officer is requested to follow their internal IT policies, which may include contacting Ciena for replacing the unit
